problem_statement: With horizon_years=2 and include_publication_year=true, reports/docs describe a 2-year window (Y through Y+1) while implementation sums 3 years (Y through Y+2) and eligibility correctly requires data through Y+2.

target_construction.horizon_years_meaning: Last year to include = publication_year + horizon_years (so when include_publication_year true, horizon_years+1 years are summed).
target_construction.include_publication_year_meaning: If true: start = publication_year; if false: start = publication_year + 1. end = publication_year + horizon_years always.

observed_semantics.target_sum_years_with_include_pub_year_t: publication_year through publication_year + horizon_years (inclusive) = horizon_years + 1 years
observed_semantics.target_sum_years_with_include_pub_year_f: publication_year+1 through publication_year + horizon_years (inclusive) = horizon_years years

worked_example.inconsistency: Reports describe 2 years; code sums 3 years.

tests.test_compute_calendar_horizon_include_pu: Asserts sum Y, Y+1, Y+2 (3 years). Passes; encodes implementation.
tests.test_is_horizon_eligible: Asserts last_year_needed = pub_year + horizon_years. Passes; encodes implementation.

diagnosis: Case A: Implementation is correct; reporting is wrong.
bug_location: Reporting and documentation only: target_profile.py (target_semantics_note and build_target_semantics_description) and README.md use (horizon_years - 1) for last year when include_publication_year is true; code uses publication_year + horizon_years (so horizon_years+1 years summed).
recommended_fix_target: Fix wording in target_profile.py and README.md to state that when include_publication_year is true, the window is publication_year through publication_year + horizon_years (inclusive), i.e. horizon_years+1 years. Do not change target or eligibility code.
